item.title: New adventure: back to computing research!
item.description: Started a new job at Sandia National Laboratories in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

item.title: VPS: Voronoi piecewise surrogate models for high-dimensional data fitting
item.description: We introduce a new method to construct credible global surrogates with local accuracy, without dictating where to sample.

item.title: Northrop Grumman: Data Science R&D Manager
item.description: Leading an interdisciplinary IRAD team on data and behavioral research.

item.title: Map-invariant spectral analysis for the identification of DNA periodicities
item.description: A novel algebraic approach to the periodicity detection problem is presented.

I am a Senior Member of Technical Staff at the Center for Computing Research of Sandia National Laboratories, in Albuquerque, New Mexico, working on computational and data science research problems, as part of the Dakota team. I have previously held R&D and management positions at Northrop Grumman Corporation in Albuquerque NM, the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering of UC Davis in Davis CA, the Computational Visualization Center under Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences at UT Austin in Austin TX, and Cisco Systems in San Jose CA.
